message:
feat(robot): add ru sync integration with robot mode support

Implements a two-pronged integration with the ru (repo updater) tool for
multi-repo synchronization workflows:

1. CLI Passthrough (ntm repo sync):
   - New `ntm repo` command group with `sync` subcommand
   - Thin wrapper that passes all flags through to `ru sync`
   - Proper error handling when ru is not installed
   - Inherits stdin/stdout/stderr for interactive use

2. Robot Mode (ntm --robot-ru-sync):
   - JSON-structured output for AI agent consumption
   - Fields: repos (synced/skipped), conflicts, duration_ms, exit_code
   - Auto-detects ru's --json capability and falls back gracefully
   - Includes stdout/stderr capture for debugging failed syncs
   - Registered in capabilities registry for discoverability

Implementation details:
- internal/cli/repo.go: New CLI command with flag passthrough
- internal/robot/ru_sync.go: Robot mode handler with JSON parsing
- Closes beads bd-1v2i1 and bd-33f7m (ru sync wrapper tasks)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

## Success Criteria
1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

## Notes
- Retention defaults to 90 days; configurable
- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1uunz","title":"Implement agent launch sequence after exit","description":"Implement agent launch sequence after successful exit.

## Steps
1. Send 'cc' Enter to pane
2. Wait for claude code initialization (6 seconds typical)
3. Verify claude code is ready (check for prompt or ready indicator)

## Timing
- cc command: immediate
- Initialization wait: 6 seconds
- Ready check: poll for up to 10 seconds

## Detection
Look for:
- Claude Code prompt character
- 'Claude Code' banner text
- Ready indicator in output

## Error Handling
- If cc command fails to start: return error with shell output
- If initialization times out: return error with pane content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:12.846348928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358201896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358098031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uunz","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v1y","title":"Task: Implement proactive compaction trigger","description":"## Purpose
Automatically trigger context compaction when prediction threshold crossed.

## Trigger Logic
if prediction.ShouldCompact && config.AutoCompact {
    // Determine agent type
    switch pane.Type {
    case AgentClaude:
        send '/compact' to pane
    case AgentCodex:
        send '!compact' to pane  // or equivalent
    case AgentGemini:
        // Gemini-specific compaction
    }
    
    // Wait for compaction to complete
    waitForCompaction(pane)
    
    // Inject recovery context
    injectRecoveryContext(pane)
}

## Scope
- Detect ShouldCompact from predictor
- Send appropriate compact command per agent type
- Wait for compaction completion
- Inject recovery context after compaction
- Notify user of automatic compaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compaction triggered before context exhaustion
- Correct command sent per agent type
- Recovery context injected post-compaction
- User notification in dashboard/alerts

## Technical Notes
Reuse session recovery infrastructure
Integrate with notification system","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1v922","title":"LimitDetector: Emit events on limit detection","description":"## LimitDetector: Emit Events on Limit Detection

### Purpose
Define the event emission system that notifies subscribers when limits are detected.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Event Channel:**
```go
// NewLimitDetector creates a LimitDetector with event channel.
func NewLimitDetector(cfg *config.SwarmConfig, tracker *ratelimit.RateLimitTracker) *LimitDetector {
    return &LimitDetector{
        Config:    cfg,
        Tracker:   tracker,
        eventChan: make(chan LimitEvent, 100), // Buffered channel
        matcher:   NewPatternMatcher(cfg.LimitPatterns),
    }
}

// Events returns the event channel for consumers.
func (d *LimitDetector) Events() <-chan LimitEvent {
    return d.eventChan
}

// Close cleans up resources.
func (d *LimitDetector) Close() {
    if d.cancel != nil {
        d.cancel()
    }
    close(d.eventChan)
}
```

**Event Consumer Pattern:**
```go
// Example: AutoRespawner consuming events
func (r *AutoRespawner) handleEvents(ctx context.Context) {
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case event, ok := <-r.LimitDetector.Events():
            if !ok {
                return // Channel closed
            }
            log.Info("[AutoRespawner] limit_received", 
                "session_pane", event.SessionPane,
                "pattern", event.Pattern)
            go r.handleLimitHit(event)
        }
    }
}
```

**JSON Output (Robot Mode):**
```go
// LimitEventOutput is the JSON format for robot mode.
type LimitEventOutput struct {
    Type        string    `json:"type"`
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`
    DetectedAt  string    `json:"detected_at"`
    RawOutput   string    `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log event creation: `[LimitDetector] event_created session_pane=%s`
- Log channel send: `[LimitDetector] event_sent session_pane=%s`
- Log channel full warning: `[LimitDetector] event_channel_full dropping=%s`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Buffered event channel (100 events)
- [ ] Non-blocking send with timeout
- [ ] Channel close on detector shutdown
- [ ] JSON serializable events for robot mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:08.602967747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:23.103730997Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:23.103355229Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v922","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v922","depends_on_id":"bd-ee3e6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vbqd","title":"CAAM: Implement caam binary detection and availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect if caam binary is available on PATH.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'caam' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify caam version is compatible
3. Check if caam has any accounts configured
4. Cache result to avoid repeated lookups

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/caam/detect.go

```go
package caam

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
)

type CAAMStatus struct {
    Available     bool
    Version       string
    AccountsCount int
    Providers     []string
}

var (
    once   sync.Once
    status CAAMStatus
)

func GetStatus() CAAMStatus {
    once.Do(detect)
    return status
}

func detect() {
    path, err := exec.LookPath("caam")
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'caam status --json' and parse
    // Populate status struct
}
```

## Testing
- Test with caam installed and accounts configured
- Test with caam installed but no accounts
- Test without caam installed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:00.050990193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:55.250507735Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:55.250364746Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vbqd","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Feature: Extend checkpoints with assignments/progress","description":"## Overview
Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkpoints remain readable even without new fields
- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled
- Restore flow surfaces assignment summary","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:12.436057119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:21.091234924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-332h","title":"Task: Implement file edit detection patterns","description":"## Purpose
Extend robot/patterns.go with patterns to detect file edits from agent output.

## Detection Patterns per Agent Type
var fileWritePatterns = map[AgentType][]string{
    AgentClaude: {
        'using the edit tool',
        'using the write tool',
        'Edited file:',
        'Created file:',
        'Writing to file:',
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'writing to file',
        'edit.*file',
        'created file',
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'Writing:',
        'Editing:',
        'Created:',
    },
}

## Path Extraction
func ExtractEditedFiles(output string, agentType AgentType) []string

Extract file paths from patterns like:
- 'Edited file: internal/cli/send.go'
- 'Writing to file /path/to/file.go'
- Tool use JSON with file_path field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Patterns detect 90%+ of file edits
- File paths extracted accurately
- Both relative and absolute paths handled
- False positives minimized

## Technical Notes
Use regexp.MustCompile for pattern matching
Test against actual agent output samples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:28.921638062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773414528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773257222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-332h","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33hgw","title":"Task: Implement RedundancyScore metric for output similarity analysis","description":"# Task: RedundancyScore Implementation

Measure similarity between mode outputs to detect unnecessary overlap.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/redundancy.go
type RedundancyScorer struct {
    Outputs []ModeOutput
}

type RedundancyReport struct {
    Overall           float64  // Average pairwise similarity
    PairwiseSimilarity []SimilarityPair
    HighRedundancy    []SimilarityPair // pairs above threshold
    Recommendations   []string
}

type SimilarityPair struct {
    ModeA      string
    ModeB      string
    Similarity float64 // 0-1
    Rationale  string  // why they're similar
}

func NewRedundancyScorer(outputs []ModeOutput) *RedundancyScorer
func (r *RedundancyScorer) Calculate() *RedundancyReport
func (r *RedundancyScorer) Render() string

// Similarity calculation
func (r *RedundancyScorer) calculatePairSimilarity(a, b ModeOutput) float64
func jaccard(setA, setB []string) float64
```

## Similarity Calculation
1. Extract finding texts from both outputs
2. Tokenize and create sets
3. Calculate Jaccard similarity
4. Weight by impact level

## Thresholds
- < 0.3: Low redundancy (good diversity)
- 0.3-0.5: Moderate redundancy (acceptable)
- > 0.5: High redundancy (overlapping insights, suggest replacement)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Calculate pairwise output similarity
- [ ] Generate overall redundancy score
- [ ] Identify high-redundancy pairs
- [ ] Suggest mode replacements for redundant pairs
- [ ] TUI-friendly render method
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T18:41:04.526378130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T18:42:34.761260493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T18:42:34.761215799Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-254do","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-33tq7","title":"Foundation: Define ProjectAllocation and SwarmPlan types","description":"## Purpose

Define the core data types that represent project allocation decisions and the overall swarm execution plan.

## Implementation

Create `internal/swarm/types.go`:

```go
package swarm

import "time"

// ProjectBeadCount represents a project and its open bead count
type ProjectBeadCount struct {
    Path      string `json:"path"`       // Absolute path to project
    Name      string `json:"name"`       // Project directory name
    OpenBeads int    `json:"open_beads"` // Count of open beads
    Tier      int    `json:"tier"`       // Calculated tier (1, 2, or 3)
}

// ProjectAllocation represents the agent allocation for a single project
type ProjectAllocation struct {
    Project     ProjectBeadCount `json:"project"`
    CCAgents    int              `json:"cc_agents"`
    CodAgents   int              `json:"cod_agents"`
    GmiAgents   int              `json:"gmi_agents"`
    TotalAgents int              `json:"total_agents"`
}

// SwarmPlan is the complete execution plan for the weighted swarm
type SwarmPlan struct {
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    ScanDir     string    `json:"scan_dir"`
    
    // Per-project allocations
    Allocations []ProjectAllocation `json:"allocations"`
    
    // Aggregate totals
    TotalCC     int `json:"total_cc"`
    TotalCod    int `json:"total_cod"`
    TotalGmi    int `json:"total_gmi"`
    TotalAgents int `json:"total_agents"`
    
    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `json:"sessions_per_type"`
    PanesPerSession int `json:"panes_per_session"` // Calculated: ceil(total/sessions)
    
    // Session names to create
    Sessions []SessionSpec `json:"sessions"`
}

// SessionSpec describes a tmux session to create
type SessionSpec struct {
    Name       string     `json:"name"`        // e.g., "cc_agents_1"
    AgentType  string     `json:"agent_type"`  // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
    PaneCount  int        `json:"pane_count"`
    Panes      []PaneSpec `json:"panes"`
}

// PaneSpec describes a pane within a session
type PaneSpec struct {
    Index       int    `json:"index"`        // 1-based pane index
    Project     string `json:"project"`      // Which project this pane works on
    AgentType   string `json:"agent_type"`
    AgentIndex  int    `json:"agent_index"`  // Agent number within project
    LaunchCmd   string `json:"launch_cmd"`   // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
}

// SwarmState tracks the runtime state of a running swarm
type SwarmState struct {
    Plan        *SwarmPlan           `json:"plan"`
    StartedAt   time.Time            `json:"started_at"`
    PaneStates  map[string]PaneState `json:"pane_states"` // key: "session:pane"
    LimitHits   []LimitHitEvent      `json:"limit_hits"`
    Respawns    []RespawnEvent       `json:"respawns"`
}

// PaneState tracks individual pane runtime state
type PaneState struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`  // "cc_agents_1:1.5"
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project       string    `json:"project"`
    Status        string    `json:"status"`        // "running", "limit_hit", "respawning"
    LastActivity  time.Time `json:"last_activity"`
    LimitHitAt    *time.Time `json:"limit_hit_at,omitempty"`
    RespawnCount  int       `json:"respawn_count"`
}

// LimitHitEvent records when an agent hits usage limits
type LimitHitEvent struct {
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project     string    `json:"project"`
    DetectedAt  time.Time `json:"detected_at"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`        // Which pattern matched
}

// RespawnEvent records agent respawns
type RespawnEvent struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    RespawnedAt   time.Time `json:"respawned_at"`
    AccountRotated bool     `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string  `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string  `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
}
```

## Design Rationale

1. **Separation of Plan vs State**: SwarmPlan is immutable after creation; SwarmState tracks runtime
2. **JSON tags**: Enable robot mode output and state persistence
3. **1-based pane indices**: Match tmux conventions (panes start at 1, not 0)
4. **Hierarchical structure**: Plan → Sessions → Panes makes orchestration straightforward

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All types defined with proper JSON tags
- [ ] Types are well-documented with godoc comments
- [ ] Unit tests for any helper methods
- [ ] Types support JSON marshaling/unmarshaling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:58:26.463622702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:40:10.203282955Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T07:40:10.203192294Z","close_reason":"Implemented all swarm types: ProjectBeadCount, ProjectAllocation, SwarmPlan, SessionSpec, PaneSpec, SwarmState, PaneState, LimitHitEvent, RespawnEvent with full JSON support and comprehensive tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33tuj","title":"Task: Add output_lines_since_last activity indicator","description":"Add output_lines_since_last field to pane status.

## What

Add `output_lines_since_last` field showing lines of output since last status check.

## Why

Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing. Useful for:
- Detecting stalled agents (0 lines for extended period)
- Detecting hyperactive agents (excessive output)
- Progress monitoring

## Implementation

Track output line count between status calls:

```go
type OutputTracker struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    lineCount  map[int]int  // pane_index -> total lines seen
}

func (t *OutputTracker) GetDelta(paneIndex int) int {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    
    current := countPaneLines(paneIndex)
    previous := t.lineCount[paneIndex]
    delta := current - previous
    t.lineCount[paneIndex] = current
    return delta
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "output_lines_since_last": 42
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- First call: return total lines (no previous)
- tmux buffer wraps: handle potential negative delta
- Agent cleared screen: reset tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:36:58.734360851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002470134Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002368092Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tuj","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,57 @@
+package cli
+
+import (
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"os/exec"
+
+	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tools"
+)
+
+func newRepoCmd() *cobra.Command {
+	cmd := &cobra.Command{
+		Use:   "repo",
+		Short: "Repo management commands",
+		Long: `Repo-level commands that pass through to external tooling.
+
+Examples:
+  ntm repo sync --dry-run     # Run ru sync in dry-run mode
+  ntm repo sync --workers=4   # Pass through ru flags as-is`,
+	}
+
+	cmd.AddCommand(newRepoSyncCmd())
+	return cmd
+}
+
+func newRepoSyncCmd() *cobra.Command {
+	cmd := &cobra.Command{
+		Use:                "sync [ru flags...]",
+		Short:              "Run ru sync (passthrough)",
+		Long:               "Thin wrapper around ru sync. All flags/args are passed through to ru.",
+		Args:               cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
+		DisableFlagParsing: true,
+		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
+			return runRepoSync(args)
+		},
+	}
+
+	return cmd
+}
+
+func runRepoSync(args []string) error {
+	adapter := tools.NewRUAdapter()
+	path, installed := adapter.Detect()
+	if !installed {
+		return fmt.Errorf("ru not installed: install ru to use 'ntm repo sync'")
+	}
+
+	ruArgs := append([]string{"sync"}, args...)
+	cmd := exec.Command(path, ruArgs...)
+	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
+	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
+	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
+
+	return cmd.Run()
+}

+package robot
+
+import (
+	"reflect"
+	"testing"
+)
+
+func TestParseRUSyncPayload(t *testing.T) {
+	cases := []struct {
+		name          string
+		payload       string
+		wantSynced    []string
+		wantSkipped   []string
+		wantConflicts []string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:          "invalid",
+			payload:       "not-json",
+			wantSynced:    []string{},
+			wantSkipped:   []string{},
+			wantConflicts: []string{},
+		},
+		{
+			name:          "top-level arrays",
+			payload:       `{"synced":["repo-a"],"skipped":["repo-b"],"conflicts":["repo-c"]}`,
+			wantSynced:    []string{"repo-a"},
+			wantSkipped:   []string{"repo-b"},
+			wantConflicts: []string{"repo-c"},
+		},
+		{
+			name:          "nested repos object",
+			payload:       `{"repos":{"synced":["repo-a"],"skipped":["repo-b"]},"conflicts":["repo-c"]}`,
+			wantSynced:    []string{"repo-a"},
+			wantSkipped:   []string{"repo-b"},
+			wantConflicts: []string{"repo-c"},
+		},
+		{
+			name:          "repos list with status",
+			payload:       `{"repos":[{"name":"repo-a","status":"synced"},{"path":"/work/repo-b","status":"skipped"},{"repo":"repo-c","status":"merge-conflict"}]}`,
+			wantSynced:    []string{"repo-a"},
+			wantSkipped:   []string{"/work/repo-b"},
+			wantConflicts: []string{"repo-c"},
+		},
+		{
+			name:          "top-level list",
+			payload:       `[{"name":"repo-a","status":"synced"},{"repo":"repo-b","status":"skipped"},{"name":"repo-c","status":"conflict"}]`,
+			wantSynced:    []string{"repo-a"},
+			wantSkipped:   []string{"repo-b"},
+			wantConflicts: []string{"repo-c"},
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range cases {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			repos, conflicts := parseRUSyncPayload([]byte(tc.payload))
+			if !reflect.DeepEqual(repos.Synced, tc.wantSynced) {
+				t.Fatalf("synced = %v, want %v", repos.Synced, tc.wantSynced)
+			}
+			if !reflect.DeepEqual(repos.Skipped, tc.wantSkipped) {
+				t.Fatalf("skipped = %v, want %v", repos.Skipped, tc.wantSkipped)
+			}
+			if !reflect.DeepEqual(conflicts, tc.wantConflicts) {
+				t.Fatalf("conflicts = %v, want %v", conflicts, tc.wantConflicts)
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
